title: This is the Green Design System blog
content:
### What you’ll find here

This blog is where we share what’s happening in the system, and how we think along the way.

You can expect:

- Changelog and updates
- Insights into design and frontend decisions
- Accessibility learnings
- Thoughts on patterns, components, and ways of working

Sometimes it will be small updates. Sometimes deeper dives. Often, a bit of both.

### A first version, built together

This is the first version of our blog, created over three days during our team days. It’s a starting point, and like everything else we build, it will continue to evolve, improve, and take shape over time.

### Why we’re sharing

We believe design systems get better when they’re open, understood, and discussed.

By sharing our work, we hope to:

- Make changes easier to follow
- Give context to decisions
- Support teams using the system
- And learn together along the way

### Join us

If you’re working with the system, or have something to share about design, code, or accessibility, we’d love to hear from you. You’re also very welcome to share feedback for the blog in the Green support channel in Teams. Let’s build better experiences, together 💚

### We are the Green team

We’re the team behind the Green Design System, a small, cross-functional team working across design and development. Joacim and Astrit, frontend developers, Jonathan and Jessi, designers, and Marwin, design director. We’re supported by Josephine, UX writer and Conny, UX designer. The team is led by Ville.

Together, we design, build, and maintain the system that supports consistent, accessible, and high-quality experiences across SEBs platforms.
